physical_meaning: Normalized reactor power / neutron flux, the primary kinetics state variable
reconciliation_notes: Core output of the point-kinetics solver; primary channel reported in the synthetic telemetry

physical_meaning: Net reactivity, sum of external (control-rod) and feedback (void + Doppler + xenon) contributions
reconciliation_notes: Decomposed into rho_rod + rho_void + rho_doppler + rho_xenon for interpretability ground-truth tagging

physical_meaning: Total fraction of fission neutrons that are delayed
reconciliation_notes: PLACEHOLDER - Keepin 1965 thermal U-235 value vs RBMK burnup-adjusted value. Reconciliation REQUIRED before freeze - see DESIGN_NOTE.md Section 4

physical_meaning: Fraction of beta attributable to precursor group i (six-group formalism)
reconciliation_notes: Six values, one per group. Source table TBD - must come from same source as decay constants (B2 or B3, not mixed)

physical_meaning: Decay constant for delayed-neutron precursor group i
reconciliation_notes: Must be taken from the same table as beta_i (paired values, not mixed across sources)

physical_meaning: Concentration of delayed-neutron precursor group i
reconciliation_notes: Six internal state variables; not necessarily exposed as a telemetry channel but available if needed for richer anomaly types

physical_meaning: Mean neutron generation time (prompt neutron lifetime / k_eff)
reconciliation_notes: Standard point-kinetics parameter; value depends on moderator (graphite vs water) - confirm graphite-moderated value from B3 rather than a water-moderated default

physical_meaning: External reactivity input from control rod / regulating system position
reconciliation_notes: Driven by the scenario script (normal operation profile + anomaly injection), not a free state variable. AZ-5/positive-scram transient explicitly NOT included - see Validation Registry V04

physical_meaning: Concentration of I-135, precursor to Xe-135 via beta decay
reconciliation_notes: State variable in the Xe/I coupled ODE pair from B4

physical_meaning: Concentration of Xe-135, dominant thermal neutron poison
reconciliation_notes: State variable in the Xe/I coupled ODE pair from B4; drives rho_xenon

physical_meaning: Reactivity contribution from Xe-135 poisoning (negative feedback)
reconciliation_notes: Computed from Xe(t) via a microscopic absorption cross-section term; this is the channel responsible for the 'iodine pit' behavior in V01

physical_meaning: Fission yield fraction for I-135 production
reconciliation_notes: Standard value; confirm against B4's specific derivation rather than a generic PWR table if RBMK fuel composition matters here

physical_meaning: Direct fission yield fraction for Xe-135 (excluding I-135 decay contribution)
reconciliation_notes: Standard value; same caveat as gamma_I

physical_meaning: Microscopic absorption cross-section of Xe-135 for thermal neutrons
reconciliation_notes: Used in rho_xenon computation; standard value but confirm thermal-spectrum assumption matches the point-kinetics model's spectrum assumptions

physical_meaning: Core-averaged coolant void fraction (fraction of coolant channel volume occupied by steam)
reconciliation_notes: Driving variable for rho_void; the 'positive void coefficient' that is RBMK's defining and most safety-relevant characteristic

physical_meaning: Reactivity contribution from changes in coolant void fraction (POSITIVE for RBMK at low ORM)
reconciliation_notes: Sign and magnitude are RBMK-specific and the most safety-critical parameter in the whole model. Magnitude per B8 (around 0.03, i.e. roughly 0.3 mk per pct void, higher near core axis) cross-checked against B9 (commonly cited ~4.7 beta pre-accident, ~0.7 beta post-modification). MUST be reviewed at Phase 4/Step 4 sanity check before freeze

physical_meaning: Reactivity per unit change in void fraction (the void coefficient itself)
units: dollars per unit void fraction (or dk/k per pct)
reconciliation_notes: See void_reactivity_feedback notes - this is the single most scrutinized parameter in the design note

physical_meaning: Reactivity contribution from fuel temperature change (Doppler broadening), always negative
reconciliation_notes: B3 provides a graphite-moderated six-group model with Doppler/graphite-temperature feedback but NO void coefficient (gas-cooled reference). Used here only for the Doppler term structure, not the void term

physical_meaning: Effective fuel temperature, drives rho_doppler
reconciliation_notes: Simplified lumped/single-node representation - full 3D thermal-hydraulics explicitly out of scope (point-kinetics simplification, see LIMITATIONS.md Section 7)

physical_meaning: Operating Reactivity Margin - count of equivalent control rods inserted, RBMK-specific safety parameter
reconciliation_notes: Used as a SCENARIO PARAMETER to set initial conditions for anomaly scenarios (e.g. low-ORM anomaly class), not as a continuously-modeled state variable in the first-pass simulator. Historical fact 'ORM was below minimum' (Anchor A-01) informs which scenario class is illustrative of the case study, but does NOT set a numeric ORM value from the digitized trace
